title: Avatar: Fire and Ash Dominates Box Office for Fourth Consecutive Weekend
text: Avatar: Fire and Ash continues its theatrical dominance, earning $21.3 million domestically during its fourth weekend at the top of the box office. The film is performing from 3,700 theaters across North America. The James Cameron-directed sequel has accumulated $342.6 million in domestic box office revenue. Globally, the film surpassed the $1 billion milestone after three weekends and is projected to reach approximately $1.23 billion worldwide following this weekend's earnings. Two new releases entered the market but neither challenged Avatar's dominance. Paramount's horror film Primate opened with $11.3 million domestically, while Lionsgate's disaster sequel Greenland 2: Migration earned $8.5 million from 2,710 locations. Though Fire and Ash hasn't matched the box office trajectories of its predecessors, the film maintains strong audience engagement. The James Cameron franchise installment is coming in well ahead of the weekend's two new releases, demonstrating sustained commercial appeal across its theatrical run.
